content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

March 27, 1990


John Santoya
4155 E. Raco
Fresno, CA 93702

Re: Your Request For Advice
Our File No. A-90-159

Dear Mr. Santoya:

You have requested advice concerning the campaign disclosure
provisions of the Political Reform Act.1/

QUESTIONS

(1) When does a group of citizens who are circulating
petitions to qualify a local ballot measure become a committee
under the Act?

(2) What disclosure requirements apply to this type of
committee?

CONCLUSION

(1) The citizens group becomes a committee when $1,000 or
more in contributions have been received to qualify an initiative,
referendum or recall measure.

(2) Ballot measure qualification committees must file a
statement of organization (Form 410) within 10 days of receiving
$1,000 or more. A campaign disclosure statement (Form 450 or
420) must be filed not later than 21 days after any petitions are
filed, or 21 days after the deadline for filing petitions,
whichever is earlier.


1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

John Santoya
Page Two

FACTS

     A group of Fresno citizens circulated petitions against the
use of water meters.  In your letter you indicated that the
petitions would have to be submitted to the city council and that
the city council would set a date for an election.  However, we
were advised by Becky Klisch, Fresno City Clerk, that the measure
is an initiative which will qualify for the ballot on the basis of
signature gathering alone.

     You advised me that the citizens group had received $1,000 or
more to qualify the measure.  As we discussed on February 27,
1990, your group qualified as a committee and was required to file
a statement of organization (Form 410) within 10 days of receiving
$1,000.


ANALYSIS

     The term "measure" includes:

          ...any proposition which is submitted or is intended to
          be submitted to a popular vote at an election by initiative,
          referendum or recall procedure whether or not it qualifies
          for the ballot.  (Section 82043.)

     Therefore, an initiative, referendum or recall becomes a
measure when the proponents begin to circulate signature petitions
to qualify the measure for the ballot.  (In re Fontana (1976) 2
FPPC Opinions 25, copy enclosed.)

     A recipient committee is any person, organization or entity
which receives contributions totaling $1,000 or more in a calendar
year.  (Section 82013.)  A statement of organization is required
to be filed with the Secretary of State within 10 days of
qualifying as a recipient committee.  Your committee must also
file a copy of the statement with the Fresno City Clerk.  (Section
84101.)

     A campaign disclosure statement (Form 420 or Form 450, copies
enclosed) must be filed not later than 21 days after any petitions
are filed, or 21 days after the deadline for filing petitions,
whichever is earlier.  (Section 84200.5(f).)  This statement must
be filed with the Fresno City Clerk.  If the measure qualifies for
the ballot, and the committee receives contributions or makes
expenditures to support passage of the measure, it will be
required to file pre-election statements during the six-month
period in which the measure is being voted upon.  In addition, the
committee will be required to file semi-annual statements which
are due on July 31st and January 31st each year until the
committee terminates.  (See Page 23 of the Information Manual on
Campaign Disclosure Provisions of the Political Reform Act, copy
enclosed.)

John Santoya
Page Three

     If you have any questions regarding this matter, please
contact me at (916) 322-5662.

                                        Sincerely,


                                        Kathryn E. Donovan
                                        General Counsel

                                        [signature: Alice Hughes]

                                        By: Alice Hughes
                                            Political Reform Consultant
                                            Technical Assistance and
                                              Analysis Division

Enclosures
